summary: This source is a letter to the editor by Lior Pachter critiquing Stephen Quake's historical account of molecular cell atlases. While it highlights Aviv Regev's central role in pioneering single-cell genomics and co-founding the Human Cell Atlas, it is primarily authored by Pachter and contains almost none of Regev's own words or detailed thought processes, aside from a brief mention of her overarching vision.

mental_models.name: Periodic table of cells
mental_models.description: A metaphor for the Human Cell Atlas, envisioning a systematic, comprehensive classification of all cell types in the human body, akin to how the periodic table organizes chemical elements.
mental_models.example: Publicizing the vision for the Human Cell Atlas project to systematically map and categorize human cells.
